detection.label: Detectability
detection.desc: Ability to detect a compromise in real time

risk_module.title: Risk Assessment IT/OT — M06
risk_module.description: Inherent = probability×impact BEFORE controls; Residual = probability×impact AFTER controls and BCP; Weighted = residual score weighted by BIA criticality to prioritise risks with the highest business impact. Risk Appetite policies define the thresholds (max acceptable score, maximum number of red risks, unacceptable score) formally approved by management.

steps.1: Create a new assessment linking the asset and BIA process (where available)
steps.2: Fill in the INHERENT risk by entering probability and impact before controls
steps.3: Fill in the RESIDUAL risk by entering probability and impact after controls and BCP measures
steps.4: Complete IT/OT dimensions and click 'Complete assessment' to calculate score, weighted score and ALE
steps.5: Compare score and weighted score with Risk Appetite thresholds to decide mitigation, transfer or acceptance
steps.6: Formally accept residual risks (when allowed by policy), providing a note and review due date

connections.bia: Weighted score and ALE use BIA criticality and cost data
connections.governance: Risk Appetite defines the thresholds approved by management
connections.pdca: Risks above threshold can open automatic PDCA cycles
connections.tasks: Mitigation tasks generated for risks beyond appetite limits

config_needed.1: Configure thresholds in Governance → Risk Appetite
config_needed.2: Link BIA processes for automatic ALE and weighted score calculation

connections.select_plant_hint: Select a site in the menu to see site-specific alerts

missing_pdca.title: {{count}} {{count, plural, one {critical risk} other {critical risks}}} without an open PDCA cycle

missing_pdca.ok: All critical risks have an open PDCA cycle

help.title: Controls library — M03
help.description: Manages regulatory controls for each framework (ISO 27001, TISAX L2/L3, NIS2). Each control has an assessment status and requires documentary evidence to reach "Compliant".

steps.1: Controls are generated automatically when a framework is loaded
steps.2: Click ▶ on a control to open the management drawer
steps.3: Link policy documents and operational evidence
steps.4: The system suggests the status automatically from linked documents
steps.5: Save the evaluation — requires at least 1 valid evidence for "Compliant"
steps.6: Download SOA (ISO27001), VDA ISA (TISAX) or NIS2 Matrix using the buttons at the top

help.title: Governance — M00
help.description: Manages normative roles (CISO, NIS2 contact, DPO, etc.) with validity periods. Expired or vacant roles trigger automatic alerts because they impact NIS2 and ISO 27001 compliance.

steps.assign_role: Assign a role to a user with a start and end date.
steps.replace_role: To replace a role owner, use "Replace" — succession is atomic.
steps.terminate_role: To terminate without a successor, use "Terminate" with a reason.
steps.vacant_roles_alert: Mandatory vacant roles (CISO, NIS2, DPO, ISMS) trigger alerts at the top.
steps.link_nomination_doc: Link the nomination document to keep formal evidence.

connections.rbac: Normative role is distinct from the application role.
connections.management_review: Vacant roles appear in the Management Review snapshot.

steps.assign_approvers: Assign roles that can approve and put documents into effect.

help.title: Incident management — M09
help.description:
Track security incidents with an integrated NIS2 timer.
Closing requires an approved RCA. On close, a PDCA and a Lesson Learned are created automatically.

steps.1: Open an incident with severity and NIS2 flag (yes/no/to be assessed)
steps.2: A 24h timer starts automatically for the preliminary NIS2 notification
steps.3: Change status to "in_analisi" and assign a team
steps.4: Fill in the RCA (Root Cause Analysis) and get it approved
steps.5: Close the incident: post-incident PDCA and Lesson Learned are created automatically

lessons.relation: Lesson learned auto-created with RCA summary

config_needed.1: Configure calculation thresholds under Incidents → NIS2 configuration; site details and NIS2 Contact in M01 / M00

nis2_classification.classify_error: Classification failed. Check the data and try again.
nis2_classification.site_non_subject: This site is not subject to NIS2 obligations.

recurrence.label: Recurrence
recurrence.auto_yes: The system detected a similar closed incident on
recurrence.auto_no: No closed precedent in the configured window (auto).

nis2_classification.override_note: Override replaces automatic calculation and is recorded in the audit trail.

method_tab.rule_fallback: PTA=max(5 axes); PTNR=PTA+recurrence bonus. Significant if PTNR≥threshold or active ACN fact pattern (site scope). M/H multipliers on base thresholds. Manual override takes precedence.
method_tab.criteria_disclaimer: The criteria list below describes inputs; the decision is not a logical OR of these rows but the PTA/PTNR engine and ACN fact patterns.
method_tab.engine_unavailable: Open the NIS2 Classification tab or retry to see the computed outcome.
method_tab.scores_snapshot_note: For up-to-date calculation use the NIS2 Classification tab; after automatic assessment these fields align.
method_tab.raw_inputs_note: For numeric axes, “≥ base threshold” only means the value reaches the configured threshold; scores 1–5 use banded multipliers (see below). Flags feed confidentiality/reputational axes.
method_tab.override_active: Manual override active: manual outcome with reason takes precedence.
method_tab.criteria_list_note: Significance is not a single-row “value ≥ threshold”: hours, euros and users become per-axis scores; then PTA, PTNR and ACN fact patterns.
method_tab.export_inputs_note: The table below shows inputs and comparison to base thresholds; the badge outcome is from the PTA/PTNR and fact-pattern engine.

config.multiplier_note: Score 3 = between threshold and threshold×M. Score 4 = between threshold×M and threshold×H. Score 5 = beyond threshold×H.
config.ptnr_note: Significant if PTNR ≥ threshold or at least one active ACN fact pattern.
config.recurrence_note: The system checks for a closed incident with the same category in the last N days at this site.

assets.criticality_1_desc: Non-critical asset. Downtime tolerated over 7 days. No BCP required.
assets.criticality_2_desc: Limited impact. Downtime tolerated 3-7 days. BCP not necessary.
assets.criticality_3_desc: Operational impact. Downtime tolerated 24-72 hours. BCP recommended.
assets.criticality_4_desc: Significant impact on production or clients. Downtime tolerated 4-24 hours. BCP mandatory.
assets.criticality_5_desc: Core asset. Downtime tolerated less than 4 hours. BCP mandatory with semi-annual testing.
assets.bia_processes_hint: Select one or more BIA processes that depend on this asset (IT or OT).

sw.empty: No software in Approved Software List

audit_prep.close_notes_placeholder: Closing notes (mandatory for Major/Minor, min 20 chars)
audit_prep.coverage_sample_long: Sample (25%) — checks priority controls in GAP or PARTIAL status
audit_prep.coverage_extended_long: Extended (50%) — balanced coverage across all domains
audit_prep.coverage_full_long: Full (100%) — all controls verified, no domain excluded
audit_prep.coverage_campione_long: Sample (25%) — checks priority controls in GAP or PARTIAL status
audit_prep.coverage_esteso_long: Extended (50%) — balanced coverage across all domains

auto_validate.tooltip: Evaluates evidence and documents on each control and opens findings for missing items
auto_validate.modal_intro: {{count}} checklist items will be evaluated. For each linked control the system sets the status and opens a minor_nc finding for missing or expired cases.
auto_validate.rule_present: Present: ≥1 valid evidence + ≥1 approved non-expired document + no open major
auto_validate.rule_expired: Expired: only expired evidence or documents → auto minor_nc to justify
auto_validate.rule_missing: Missing: no valid evidence and no document → auto minor_nc
auto_validate.rule_idempotent: Idempotent: if an open finding already exists on the same control it is not duplicated
auto_validate.finding_auto_tooltip: Finding generated automatically by prep validation — review and justify if still open
auto_validate.warning_missing_extended_body: The prep targets {{requested}} but contains no EvidenceItem for frameworks {{missing}}. Validation may therefore overlook controls already fulfilled at the lower level. Use "Sync extended framework controls" and re-run.

sync_controls.tooltip: Adds EvidenceItem records for L2 (and PROTO→L2+L3) controls still missing in the prep, per TISAX hierarchy

audit_prep.cannot_complete: Cannot complete with {{count}} open Major NC
audit_prep.cancel_prep_modal_desc: Reason (min 10 characters). Open findings will be closed.
audit_prep.coverage_hint: Defines how many controls to verify. You can customise it per quarter in step 3.
audit_prep.multi_fw_hint: A single audit covers all selected frameworks in the same quarter
audit_prep.generate_plan_hint: Generate the suggested plan based on open gaps
audit_prep.plan_priority_hint: The system prioritised domains with the most controls in GAP or PARTIAL status.
audit_prep.no_program_desc: The annual program defines the audits to run in each quarter. Create the first one to plan the year.
audit_prep.select_site_topbar: Select a site in the topbar
audit_prep.delete_prep_desc: Irreversible operation. Findings will be deleted.
audit_prep.delete_program_desc: Irreversible operation. The annual program will be deleted.
audit_prep.help_description: Manages the annual audit program and individual audit execution. The program plans what to audit each quarter; preps are the concrete execution.
audit_prep.help_step1: Create the Annual Program with the wizard (start of year)
audit_prep.help_step2: The system suggests domains to audit based on open gaps
audit_prep.help_step3: Before the quarter: click 'Launch audit' to open the prep
audit_prep.help_step4: During the audit: fill in evidence and add findings
audit_prep.help_step7: The program automatically updates the % completion

osint.title: OSINT Monitor
osint.subtitle: Attack surface and domain reputation monitoring

tooltip.dnssec: DNSSEC cryptographically signs DNS responses to prevent DNS hijacking and cache poisoning. Without DNSSEC an attacker can redirect traffic to malicious servers.
tooltip.spf: Sender Policy Framework: a DNS record listing SMTP servers authorized to send mail for the domain. Missing SPF (or +all) lets anyone spoof the domain in phishing campaigns.
tooltip.dmarc: Domain-based Message Authentication: tells receiving mail servers what to do with messages that fail SPF/DKIM. Missing DMARC leaves the domain open to phishing and business email compromise.

findings.dmarc_none: DMARC present but policy = none (no protection)
findings.enricher_error: Partial data: enrichers {{names}} did not complete
findings.ssl_unreachable: Port 443 unreachable on {{domain}} — no SSL certificate detected
findings.ssl_no_https: No HTTPS detected on {{domain}} — domain does not expose web services (normal for email-only or internal domains)

ai.title: AI Analysis
ai.description: The AI will analyze collected OSINT data to generate a detailed report. Data is anonymized before sending.

settings.anonymization_hint: Domains and sensitive data are replaced with placeholders before being sent to the AI. Recommended.
